message:
fix(tests): stabilize flaky IRC uptime and sliding window timing tests

irc/session.rs:
- Replace racy uptime_is_positive test that compared nanos > 0 with a
  deterministic approach: call uptime() to verify no panic, sleep 2ms,
  then assert uptime >= 1ms. The original test could flake when the
  system clock granularity was coarser than nanoseconds.

fcp-ratelimit/sliding_window.rs:
- Widen timing margins in sliding_window_partial_expiry from 100ms
  window / 20ms gaps to 1000ms window / 200ms gaps. The tight 20ms
  gaps were susceptible to scheduling jitter on loaded CI machines,
  causing the first request to not yet be expired when the test
  expected it. With 200ms gaps there is clear separation between
  expiry times.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
